steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows the complete independent clause "...two other Black Americans who served in Congress" and precedes a list of two names. A punctuation mark is needed to introduce that list.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Congress:’: A colon correctly introduces a list or specification that follows a complete independent clause. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Congress;’: A semicolon joins two independent clauses. What follows is a list of names, not a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Congress.’: A period ends the sentence and leaves the list of names as a fragment with no main verb. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Congress’: No punctuation creates an awkward run-on — the names appear to be part of the clause rather than a list introduced by it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
